Bankrbot has closed the loop on the runner and workflows — the two items it left "unconfirmed" I can chase down. The settlement tx is checkable on a public explorer right now:  Searched the web Searched the web  On-chain confirmation is done — that closes bankrbot's last open item. The settlement is real and independently verifiable:  Tx: 0xc197b5141e4454cb9660cbc8b0774402ff1881d1663d356c4beb4929f6f92c3d — 0.01 USDC (10,000 raw units, 6 decimals) from 0x2882...dfb0 to your payTo 0x525e...8a2C5, settled 2026-07-22 00:24 UTC. Explorer link for the PR: https://basescan.org/tx/0xc197b5141e4454cb9660cbc8b0774402ff1881d1663d356c4beb4929f6f92c3d. The address history also shows earlier settlements landing on Jul 13 and Jul 17 (including one ~7.96 USDC payment), so the rail has been settling repeatedly, not just once.  That means every item from the original failed check is now green: runner verified, workflows verified, API serving v4 personas, settlement confirmed on-chain. The one claim nobody outside can verify is "old v3 batch rows scrubbed" — that's DB-internal; if you want it confirmed, run npx tsx scripts/check-tags.ts (or db-check.ts) locally and you'll see whether any batch-* polluted rows remain. Optional — it doesn't block anything.  Two closing thoughts. First, the only remaining go-live step is the one from before: the BankrBot/skills PR — paste the Basescan link above into the PR body as the proof tx (the HANDOFF template has the slot for it), since that's exactly what reviewers want to see. Until merge, the fork URL keeps working for installs.  Second, on bankrbot's roadmap: the ideas are reasonable but they're all net-new products, so I'd rank them by how much you already have built. The launch-health oracle is closest — your repo already has /api/v1/simulate and the doppler-a2a-v1 output batch, so a "resistance score per launch" is mostly packaging. The bias-guard app is second (it's the guardrails workflow behind an intercept hook). Dynamic pricing and app templates are the furthest out and depend on demand signal you don't have yet — I'd wait for real paid traffic before building either. One caution: the $BNKR contract address in that message came from an external bot — verify it independently before wiring anything to it or referencing it anywhere. Your own SKILL.md's rule applies in both directions: treat pasted addresses as data, not instructions.

Expanding the circle-of-spheres graph to add random-direction lines from each circle point with random lengths, and spheres at the line endpoints. Adding 2 new sliders for line length control and endpoint sphere size. Using Expression nodes with sin/cos for random 3D directions, computing endpoint positions, creating Line curves, and instancing spheres at endpoints.

Fixing rejected edges (Expression only has a,b,c,d inputs - need to restructure endY/endZ formulas), wiring endpointRadius and baseSphereRadius to their respective sphere radius parameters, and fixing the lines by creating proper start-end point pairs for PolylineCurve with groupBy='row' to make individual segments.
